description_long: This is a brick house with a prominent gable roof covered in a muted, light brown material. The front facade features a large, central triangular window arrangement above the main entry, flanked by two rectangular windows, and a covered porch supported by brick columns. The side and rear elevations display a simpler window pattern, with the rear showing four evenly spaced windows and small attic vents. The house sits on a concrete foundation and includes a side door and a partially enclosed porch area. The roof is steeply pitched with visible chimneys or vent pipes protruding from the surface. The overall design is functional and symmetrical, with a focus on brickwork and large window openings for natural light.
description: A brick house with a steep gable roof, large front windows, and a covered porch supported by columns. The design is symmetrical and features prominent brickwork and a concrete foundation.
description_view_0: This view shows the side of the house with a plain brick wall, a single door, a small window, and the covered porch area.
description_view_1: This view highlights the gable end with the covered porch, brick columns, and the underside of the roof structure.
description_view_2: This is the front view, featuring the main entry, large triangular window arrangement, two rectangular windows, and the covered porch.
description_view_3: This rear view displays four evenly spaced windows and small attic vents near the roof peak.
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical rows with a large central triangular window
attribute_entryway_design: Main entry with a covered porch, brick columns, and a prominent overhang
